mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are negatively correlated with RSAFS monthly growth (Rank IC < 0) over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) crosses below 7.5%, it signals sustained improvement in labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses below 7.5% the average forward 60-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over the subsequent 3 years, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low inflation (CPIAUCSL) and low policy rates (FEDFUNDS) indicate an accommodative monetary stance that reduces discount rates and supports equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If when both FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year < 2.5%, the average forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any 3-year period, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a low inflation regime (CPIAUCSL year-over-year < 2.5%), rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects solid supply growth without overheating, which raises corporate earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 forward returns higher over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If during low inflation periods the correlation between INDPRO monthly growth and forward 60-day returns is not significantly positive (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year window, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 900 thousand units, it signals strengthening residential investment and broader economic momentum, which boosts corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after HOUST exceeds 900, the average forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year period, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates strong labor market momentum, which raises consumer income and spending, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If when PAYEMS increases month-over-month and UNRATE declines over the same month, the average forward 20-day return is not positive over any 2-year window, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) serves as a real-time proxy for economic activity; positive growth signals rising output and capacity utilization, which boosts short-term earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC between INDPRO monthly growth and forward 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: During the zero interest rate policy regime (FEDFUNDS <= 0.5%), the federal funds rate is at a level that signals extreme accommodation; as long as rates remain at this low level, the discount rate on equities is compressed, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during ZIRP regime the average forward 20-day return is not positive, or if an increase in FEDFUNDS (still <=0.5%) is associated with positive returns, hypothesis rejected.
